title: Zodiac Killer
description:
Unidentified serial killer who operated in Northern California in the late 1960s and early 1970s. The killer sent taunting letters and cryptograms to newspapers. At least five confirmed victims.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zodiac_Killer)

title: BTK Killer - Dennis Rader
description:
Dennis Rader murdered ten people in Sedgwick County, Kansas between 1974 and 1991. He called himself BTK (Bind, Torture, Kill) and sent taunting communications to police and media. Arrested in 2005.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dennis_Rader)

title: Ted Bundy
description:
Ted Bundy was a serial killer who kidnapped, raped, and murdered numerous young women across multiple states during the 1970s. He confessed to 30 murders before his execution in 1989.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ted_Bundy)

title: John Wayne Gacy
description:
John Wayne Gacy sexually assaulted and murdered at least 33 young men and boys in Cook County, Illinois between 1972 and 1978. Known as the Killer Clown, he buried most victims in the crawl space of his home.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Wayne_Gacy)

title: Son of Sam - David Berkowitz
description:
David Berkowitz terrorized New York City from 1976 to 1977, shooting six people dead and wounding seven others. He sent letters to police and newspapers signing as Son of Sam. Arrested in August 1977.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Berkowitz)

title: Black Dahlia Murder
description:
Elizabeth Short, known as the Black Dahlia, was found murdered and mutilated in Los Angeles on January 15, 1947. Her body had been severed at the waist. The case remains one of the oldest unsolved murders in Los Angeles.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_Dahlia)

title: Andrea Yates
description:
Andrea Yates drowned her five children in the bathtub of her Houston home on June 20, 2001. She was found not guilty by reason of insanity in a 2006 retrial and committed to a state mental hospital.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrea_Yates)

title: Jack the Ripper
description:
Unidentified serial killer who murdered and mutilated at least five women in the Whitechapel district of London in 1888. The case is one of the most famous unsolved murder mysteries in history.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_the_Ripper)

title: Fred and Rosemary West
description:
Fred and Rosemary West tortured, raped, and murdered at least 12 young women between 1967 and 1987 at their home at 25 Cromwell Street, Gloucester. Fred committed suicide in prison; Rose was convicted in 1995.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fred_West)

title: Bible John
description:
Bible John is the nickname of an unidentified serial killer who murdered three young women after meeting them at the Barrowland Ballroom in Glasgow between 1968 and 1969. He reportedly quoted scripture.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bible_John)

title: Harold Shipman
description:
Harold Shipman was a British doctor and one of the most prolific serial killers in recorded history. He murdered an estimated 250 patients by administering lethal doses of diamorphine. Convicted in 2000.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harold_Shipman)

title: Fritz Haarmann - The Butcher of Hanover
description:
Fritz Haarmann murdered at least 24 boys and young men in Hanover between 1918 and 1924. Known as the Butcher of Hanover, he bit through his victims' throats and allegedly sold their flesh as meat. Executed in 1925.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fritz_Haarmann)

title: Peter Kurten - The Vampire of Dusseldorf
description:
Peter Kurten committed a series of murders and sexual assaults in Dusseldorf in 1929. Known as the Vampire of Dusseldorf due to his practice of drinking victims' blood. Convicted and executed in 1931.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_K%C3%BCrten)

title: Armin Meiwes - The Rotenburg Cannibal
description:
Armin Meiwes killed and consumed a voluntary victim, Bernd Brandes, whom he found via the internet. The case drew worldwide attention and raised legal questions. Meiwes was convicted of murder in 2006.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armin_Meiwes)

title: Wolfgang Priklopil - Natascha Kampusch Kidnapping
description:
Ten-year-old Natascha Kampusch was kidnapped in Vienna by Wolfgang Priklopil and held captive in a cellar for over eight years. She escaped in 2006; Priklopil committed suicide the same day.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natascha_Kampusch)

title: Juergen Bartsch
description:
Juergen Bartsch murdered four boys between 1962 and 1966 in the Ruhr area of Germany. He lured them into an abandoned air-raid shelter, where he tortured and killed them. Died in 1976 during voluntary castration surgery.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/J%C3%BCrgen_Bartsch)

title: Landru - The Bluebeard of Gambais
description:
Henri Desire Landru lured at least 10 women through lonely-hearts advertisements, murdered them for their assets, and incinerated their remains in his villa near Paris. Guillotined in 1922.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henri_D%C3%A9sir%C3%A9_Landru)

title: Guy Georges - The Beast of the Bastille
description:
Guy Georges was a French serial killer who raped and murdered at least seven women in eastern Paris between 1991 and 1997. He was identified through DNA evidence and sentenced to life imprisonment in 2001.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guy_Georges)

title: Emile Louis
description:
Emile Louis was a French serial killer convicted in 2004 of murdering seven young disabled women in the Yonne department between 1977 and 1979. He was a bus driver who transported his victims. Died in prison in 2013.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89mile_Louis)

title: Michel Fourniret - The Ogre of the Ardennes
description:
Michel Fourniret kidnapped, raped, and murdered at least seven girls and young women across France and Belgium between 1987 and 2001. Convicted in 2008 and sentenced to life. Died in prison in 2021.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michel_Fourniret)

title: Tsutomu Miyazaki - The Otaku Murderer
description:
Tsutomu Miyazaki abducted, murdered, and mutilated four young girls between 1988 and 1989 in Saitama and Tokyo prefectures. Known as the Otaku Murderer and the Dracula Killer. Executed in 2008.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tsutomu_Miyazaki)

title: Akihabara Massacre
description:
Tomohiro Kato drove a truck into a crowd in Akihabara, Tokyo, then stabbed pedestrians, killing seven people and injuring ten. He was convicted and executed in 2022.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akihabara_massacre)

title: Glico-Morinaga Case
description:
A group calling themselves The Monster with 21 Faces kidnapped the president of Glico and extorted several Japanese candy companies by poisoning products on store shelves. The case remains unsolved after the statute of limitations expired in 2000.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glico-Morinaga_case)

title: Ivan Milat - Backpacker Murders
description:
Ivan Milat murdered seven backpackers in the Belanglo State Forest, New South Wales, between 1989 and 1992. The victims were from Australia, the UK, and Germany. Convicted in 1996, died in prison in 2019.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivan_Milat)

title: Peter Falconio Disappearance
description:
British tourist Peter Falconio was murdered on a remote stretch of the Stuart Highway in the Northern Territory. His body was never found. Bradley John Murdoch was convicted of his murder in 2005.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_Falconio)

title: Melbourne Gangland Killings
description:
A series of gangland murders in Melbourne between 1998 and 2010 involving rival drug syndicates. At least 36 people were killed. Key figures included Carl Williams, who was convicted then murdered in prison in 2010.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melbourne_gangland_killings)

title: Beaumont Children Disappearance
description:
Jane, Arnna, and Grant Beaumont disappeared from Glenelg Beach in Adelaide on January 26, 1966. The case remains one of Australia's most famous unsolved crimes.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disappearance_of_the_Beaumont_children)

title: Robert Pickton
description:
Robert Pickton was a Canadian serial killer convicted of the second-degree murders of six women from Vancouver's Downtown Eastside. He was charged in connection with the deaths of an additional 20 women. Convicted in 2007.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Pickton)

title: Paul Bernardo and Karla Homolka
description:
Paul Bernardo and Karla Homolka kidnapped, sexually assaulted, and murdered at least three young women in Ontario, including Homolka's sister. Known as the Ken and Barbie Killers. Bernardo was sentenced to life in 1995.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Bernardo)

title: Luka Magnotta
description:
Luka Magnotta murdered Jun Lin in Montreal, filmed the killing, and mailed body parts to political party offices and schools. He fled to Europe and was captured in Berlin. Convicted of first-degree murder in 2014.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luka_Magnotta)

title: Pedrinho Matador
description:
Pedro Rodrigues Filho, known as Pedrinho Matador, is one of Brazil's most notorious serial killers, claiming to have killed over 70 people. He murdered his first victims at age 14 and targeted other criminals in prison. Released in 2007.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pedro_Rodrigues_Filho)

title: Suzane von Richthofen Case
description:
Suzane von Richthofen conspired with her boyfriend Daniel Cravinhos to murder her wealthy parents in Sao Paulo while they slept. The case shocked Brazil. She was convicted in 2006 and sentenced to 39 years.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suzane_von_Richthofen)

title: Marielle Franco Assassination
description:
Marielle Franco, a Rio de Janeiro city councillor and human rights activist, was assassinated along with her driver Anderson Gomes. The politically motivated killing drew international condemnation. Suspects were arrested in 2019.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Marielle_Franco)

title: Juana Barraza - The Old Lady Killer
description:
Juana Barraza was a Mexican professional wrestler and serial killer who murdered at least 11 elderly women in Mexico City between 1998 and 2006 by strangling them. She was sentenced to 759 years in prison.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Juana_Barraza)

title: 43 Ayotzinapa Students Disappearance
description:
43 male students from the Ayotzinapa Rural Teachers' College were forcibly abducted and disappeared in Iguala, Guerrero. The mass disappearance triggered widespread protests. Investigation ongoing with government involvement alleged.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014_Iguala_mass_kidnapping)

title: Andres Munoz Jimenez - The Sadist of Colonia Roma
description:
Gregorio Cardenas Hernandez, known as the Strangler of Tacuba, murdered four women in Mexico City in 1942. He buried their bodies in his garden. He was convicted but later released and became a lawyer. One of Mexico's most infamous cases.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gregorio_C%C3%A1rdenas_Hern%C3%A1ndez)

title: 2008 Mumbai Attacks
description:
Ten members of Lashkar-e-Taiba carried out coordinated shooting and bombing attacks across Mumbai over four days, killing 175 people and wounding over 300. The sole surviving attacker, Ajmal Kasab, was executed in 2012.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008_Mumbai_attacks)

title: Nirbhaya Gang Rape Case
description:
A 23-year-old woman was gang-raped and fatally assaulted on a moving bus in Delhi. The brutality of the attack sparked massive protests and led to significant changes in Indian rape laws. Four of the six accused were executed in 2020.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_Delhi_gang_rape_and_murder)

title: Charles Sobhraj - The Serpent
description:
Charles Sobhraj was a serial killer who preyed on Western tourists along the Asian hippie trail in the 1970s. He operated across multiple countries and was linked to at least 12 murders. Imprisoned in Nepal, released in 2022.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Sobhraj)

title: Andrei Chikatilo - The Butcher of Rostov
description:
Andrei Chikatilo was a Soviet serial killer who murdered at least 52 women and children between 1978 and 1990 in the Rostov region and other areas. Known as the Butcher of Rostov. Convicted in 1992, executed in 1994.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrei_Chikatilo)

title: Alexander Pichushkin - The Chessboard Killer
description:
Alexander Pichushkin murdered at least 48 people in Moscow's Bitsa Park between 2001 and 2006. His goal was to fill all 64 squares of a chessboard. Convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment in 2007.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Pichushkin)

title: Assassination of Boris Nemtsov
description:
Russian opposition leader Boris Nemtsov was shot dead on a bridge near the Kremlin in Moscow. Five Chechen men were convicted of the murder in 2017, but critics allege the masterminds were never identified.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Boris_Nemtsov)

title: Gao Chengyong - Chinese Jack the Ripper
description:
Gao Chengyong raped and murdered 11 women and girls in Gansu and Inner Mongolia provinces between 1988 and 2002. Called China's Jack the Ripper, he was identified through DNA in 2016 and executed in 2019.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gao_Chengyong)

title: Yang Xinhai
description:
Yang Xinhai murdered 67 people and raped 23 women across four Chinese provinces between 2000 and 2003. He broke into homes at night and attacked entire families. Executed in 2004.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yang_Xinhai)

title: Shanghai Serial Poisoner - Wang Yonglai
description:
Zhu Jun poisoned and killed her husband and several other people using tetramine rat poison in Shanghai. The case exposed lax regulation of toxic substances in China. She was convicted and executed.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poisoning_in_China)

title: Monster of Florence
description:
An unidentified serial killer murdered 16 people, mostly couples, in the province of Florence between 1968 and 1985. The killer shot victims and performed mutilations. Despite multiple suspects and trials, the case remains officially unsolved.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monster_of_Florence)

title: Donato Bilancia
description:
Donato Bilancia was an Italian serial killer who murdered 17 people in Liguria between 1997 and 1998, including prostitutes, train passengers, and others. He was sentenced to 13 consecutive life terms.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donato_Bilancia)

title: Kidnapping of Aldo Moro
description:
Former Italian Prime Minister Aldo Moro was kidnapped by the Red Brigades in Rome. After 55 days of captivity and failed negotiations, he was murdered. His body was found in the trunk of a car. Multiple members were convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_and_murder_of_Aldo_Moro)

title: ETA Assassination of Luis Carrero Blanco
description:
Spanish Prime Minister Luis Carrero Blanco was assassinated in Madrid by the Basque separatist group ETA in Operation Ogro. A bomb planted in a tunnel beneath the street launched his car over a five-story building.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Luis_Carrero_Blanco)

title: 2004 Madrid Train Bombings
description:
Coordinated bombings on commuter trains in Madrid killed 193 people and wounded around 2,000. The attacks were carried out by an al-Qaeda-inspired terrorist cell. Multiple perpetrators were convicted in 2007.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2004_Madrid_train_bombings)

title: Marta del Castillo Case
description:
17-year-old Marta del Castillo was murdered by her ex-boyfriend Miguel Carcano in Seville. Her body has never been found despite extensive searches. Carcano was convicted and sentenced to 21 years.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Marta_del_Castillo)

title: Oscar Pistorius - Murder of Reeva Steenkamp
description:
Paralympic athlete Oscar Pistorius shot and killed his girlfriend Reeva Steenkamp through a locked bathroom door at his home in Pretoria. He claimed he mistook her for an intruder. Convicted of murder in 2015.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trial_of_Oscar_Pistorius)

title: Moses Sithole - The ABC Killer
description:
Moses Sithole raped and murdered at least 38 women in Gauteng province between 1994 and 1995. Known as the ABC Killer for targeting victims in Atteridgeville, Boksburg, and Cleveland. Sentenced to 2,410 years in prison.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moses_Sithole)

title: Murders of the Van Breda Family
description:
Henri van Breda attacked his family with an axe at their estate in Stellenbosch, killing his parents and brother and severely injuring his sister. He claimed an intruder was responsible. Convicted of three counts of murder in 2018.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Van_Breda_family_murders)

title: AMIA Bombing
description:
A car bomb attack on the Argentine Israelite Mutual Association building in Buenos Aires killed 85 people and injured hundreds. It was the deadliest bombing in Argentine history. Iran has been accused of involvement but no one has been convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMIA_bombing)

title: Murder of Maria Soledad Morales
description:
17-year-old Maria Soledad Morales was found murdered in Catamarca province. Her case exposed corruption linking her killers to powerful political families. The case triggered massive protests known as the Marches of Silence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Mar%C3%ADa_Soledad_Morales)

title: Cromagnon Nightclub Fire
description:
A fire at the Republica Cromagnon nightclub in Buenos Aires killed 194 people, mostly young concertgoers. The fire was caused by a flare that ignited flammable soundproofing. The club manager Omar Chaban was sentenced to 20 years.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rep%C3%BAblica_Croma%C3%B1%C3%B3n_nightclub_fire)

title: Yoo Young-chul
description:
Yoo Young-chul was a South Korean serial killer who murdered 21 people in Seoul between September 2003 and July 2004. He targeted wealthy elderly people and sex workers. Sentenced to death in 2005.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yoo_Young-chul)

title: Hwaseong Serial Murders
description:
Between 1986 and 1991, ten women were raped and murdered in Hwaseong, Gyeonggi Province. The case inspired the film Memories of Murder. In 2019, DNA evidence identified Lee Chun-jae, already imprisoned for another murder, as the killer.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hwaseong_serial_murders)

title: Sewol Ferry Disaster Criminal Case
description:
The ferry MV Sewol capsized off the coast of South Korea, killing 304 people, mostly high school students. The captain and crew were convicted of negligence. Captain Lee Joon-seok was sentenced to life in prison for murder.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_MV_Sewol)

title: Assassination of Olof Palme
description:
Swedish Prime Minister Olof Palme was shot and killed while walking home from a cinema in Stockholm. In 2020, prosecutors named Stig Engstrom as the prime suspect, but he died in 2000. The case was officially closed without a conviction.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Olof_Palme)

title: Thomas Quick (Sture Bergwall)
description:
Sture Bergwall, using the alias Thomas Quick, confessed to over 30 murders across Scandinavia while in a psychiatric facility. He was convicted of eight murders but later recanted all confessions. All convictions were overturned between 2009 and 2013.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sture_Bergwall)

title: Murder of Anna Lindh
description:
Swedish Foreign Minister Anna Lindh was stabbed while shopping at a department store in Stockholm. She died the following day. Mijailo Mijailovic was convicted of her murder and sentenced to life imprisonment.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Anna_Lindh)

title: Birgitte Tengs Murder
description:
17-year-old Birgitte Tengs was found murdered on the island of Karmoy, Norway. Her cousin was acquitted of the crime. In 2021, Johny Vassbakk was arrested based on new DNA evidence and was convicted in 2023.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Birgitte_Tengs)

title: Orderud Case
description:
Three members of the Orderud family were shot dead at the Orderud farm near Oslo. Per Orderud, his wife Veronica, and two accomplices were convicted. The case was driven by a dispute over inheritance and the farm estate.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orderud_case)

title: 2011 Norway Attacks - Anders Breivik
description:
Anders Behring Breivik carried out a bombing in Oslo that killed 8 people, then shot and killed 69 people at a youth camp on Utoya island. It was the deadliest attack in Norway since World War II. Sentenced to 21 years preventive detention.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_Norway_attacks)

title: Heineken Kidnapping
description:
Beer magnate Freddy Heineken and his chauffeur Ab Doderer were kidnapped in Amsterdam and held for 21 days until a ransom of 35 million guilders was paid. The five kidnappers, led by Cor van Hout and Willem Holleeder, were eventually captured.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_of_Freddy_Heineken)

title: Assassination of Pim Fortuyn
description:
Dutch politician Pim Fortuyn was shot and killed in a parking lot in Hilversum shortly before the general election. Volkert van der Graaf, an environmental activist, was convicted and sentenced to 18 years. Released in 2014.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Pim_Fortuyn)

title: Murder of Theo van Gogh
description:
Dutch filmmaker Theo van Gogh was murdered in Amsterdam by Mohammed Bouyeri, a Dutch-Moroccan Islamist extremist, in retaliation for Van Gogh's film Submission. Bouyeri was sentenced to life imprisonment.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Theo_van_Gogh)

title: Marc Dutroux Case
description:
Marc Dutroux kidnapped six girls in Belgium, four of whom died. The case triggered widespread outrage over police failures and alleged cover-ups. Dutroux was convicted in 2004 and sentenced to life imprisonment.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marc_Dutroux)

title: Brabant Killers
description:
A gang known as the Brabant Killers carried out a series of violent robberies in Belgian supermarkets between 1982 and 1985, killing 28 people. The attackers seemed more interested in killing than stealing. The case remains unsolved.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brabant_killers)

title: 2016 Brussels Bombings
description:
Three coordinated suicide bombings struck Brussels airport and Maalbeek metro station, killing 32 people and injuring over 300. The attacks were carried out by an ISIL terrorist cell. Nine suspects were convicted in 2023.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Brussels_bombings)

title: Josef Fritzl Case
description:
Josef Fritzl imprisoned his daughter Elisabeth in a concealed basement in Amstetten, Austria, for 24 years. He fathered seven children with her. The case was discovered in 2008. Fritzl was sentenced to life imprisonment.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fritzl_case)

title: Jack Unterweger
description:
Jack Unterweger was an Austrian serial killer and writer who murdered prostitutes in Austria, Czechoslovakia, and the United States. He became a literary celebrity while in prison. Convicted in 1994, he committed suicide the same night.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Unterweger)

title: Bruno Lanz Murders
description:
Franz Leitner shot and killed his ex-wife and three other people in a rampage at a party in Annaberg, Lower Austria, before fleeing into the mountains. He was found dead by suicide after a massive manhunt.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Annaberg_shooting)

title: Rupperswil Quadruple Murder
description:
Thomas N. murdered four members of a family in Rupperswil, Aargau, and set their house on fire. The 33-year-old man was motivated by sexual sadism. He was convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment in 2018.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rupperswil_quadruple_murder)

title: Zug Parliament Shooting
description:
Friedrich Leibacher entered the parliament of the canton of Zug and opened fire, killing 14 politicians and journalists before killing himself. It remains the worst mass shooting in Swiss history.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zug_massacre)

title: Murder of Lucie Trezzini
description:
Lucie Trezzini, a 29-year-old social worker, was murdered in her apartment in Zurich. Her flatmate and his girlfriend were convicted of the brutal killing. The case drew significant media attention in Switzerland.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Lucie_Trezzini)

title: Leszek Pekalski
description:
Leszek Pekalski confessed to over 70 murders of women across Poland, though he was only convicted of one. Active from the 1980s to the 1990s, he targeted women living alone in rural areas. Sentenced to 25 years in prison.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leszek_P%C4%99kalski)

title: Kidnapping of Krzysztof Olewnik
description:
Polish businessman Krzysztof Olewnik was kidnapped and held captive for over two years. Despite a ransom being paid, he was murdered by his captors. The case exposed widespread police corruption and incompetence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_of_Krzysztof_Olewnik)

title: Karol Kot - The Vampire of Krakow
description:
Karol Kot was a teenage serial killer in Krakow who committed multiple attacks between 1964 and 1966, stabbing and attempting to kill several people including children. Known as the Vampire of Krakow. Executed in 1968 at age 19.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karol_Kot)

title: Orlicky Vrah - The Orcadian Killer
description:
A series of unsolved murders of young women in the Orlicke Mountains region of the Czech Republic in the early 1990s. Multiple victims were found in wooded areas. Despite extensive investigation, no one was convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Czech_Republic)

title: Murder of Anezka Hruzova
description:
The murder of Anezka Hruzova in Polna led to the infamous Hilsner Affair, in which Leopold Hilsner was falsely accused based on antisemitic blood libel. The case became a major controversy with parallels to the Dreyfus Affair.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hilsner_Affair)

title: Ivan Roubal
description:
Ivan Roubal was a Czech serial killer who murdered at least four people in Prague in the early 1990s. He was a taxi driver who killed his victims for financial gain. Sentenced to life imprisonment.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivan_Roubal)

title: Serial Killer of Istanbul - Atalay Filiz
description:
Atalay Filiz was a Turkish academic who murdered at least three people, including his ex-girlfriend and another couple. He evaded capture for months before being arrested in Izmir in 2016. Sentenced to three aggravated life terms.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atalay_Filiz)

title: Hrant Dink Assassination
description:
Turkish-Armenian journalist Hrant Dink was assassinated outside his newspaper office in Istanbul. The killing drew international condemnation and massive protests. The gunman Ogun Samast was convicted, and a wider conspiracy was uncovered.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Hrant_Dink)

title: Yavuz Yapicioglu Serial Murders
description:
Turkish serial killer known for a series of murders in Istanbul in the early 2000s targeting women. The case highlighted issues with police response to violence against women in Turkey.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Turkey)

title: Raya and Sakina
description:
Raya and Sakina were two sisters who, along with their husbands, murdered at least 17 women in Alexandria, Egypt, in 1920. They lured victims to their home and strangled them for their jewelry. Both sisters were executed in 1921.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raya_and_Sakina)

title: Assassination of Anwar Sadat
description:
Egyptian President Anwar Sadat was assassinated during a military parade in Cairo by Islamist extremists led by Khalid Islambouli. The assassination was motivated by Sadat's peace treaty with Israel. Islambouli was executed in 1982.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Anwar_Sadat)

title: The Tanta Killer - Ahmad Sawi
description:
Ahmad Sawi was an Egyptian serial killer who murdered several women in the Gharbia governorate. He confessed to the killings and was sentenced to death. The case highlighted safety concerns in rural Egypt.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Egypt)

title: Evans Birnbaum - The Otokoto Case
description:
An 11-year-old boy named Ikechukwu Okonkwo was murdered in Owerri, Nigeria, in what was alleged to be a ritual killing connected to Chief Vincent Duru. The discovery of the boy's severed head sparked riots. Multiple suspects were convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otokoto_case)

title: Clifford Orji - The Lagos Cannibal
description:
Clifford Orji was arrested in Lagos after being found under a bridge with human remains and body parts. He was accused of killing and cannibalizing multiple victims. He died in prison in 2012 while awaiting trial.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clifford_Orji)

title: Kidnapping of Chibok Schoolgirls
description:
276 female students were kidnapped from the Government Girls Secondary School in Chibok by Boko Haram militants. The mass kidnapping sparked the global BringBackOurGirls campaign. As of 2024, over 100 girls remain missing.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chibok_schoolgirls_kidnapping)

title: Murder of Julie Ward
description:
British wildlife photographer Julie Ward was found dead in the Masai Mara game reserve. Her dismembered and partially burned body was initially dismissed as an animal attack. Despite two murder trials, no one was convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Julie_Ward)

title: 2013 Westgate Shopping Mall Attack
description:
Al-Shabaab militants attacked the Westgate shopping mall in Nairobi, killing 67 people and injuring over 175 during a four-day siege. The attack was claimed as retaliation for Kenyan military operations in Somalia.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westgate_shopping_mall_attack)

title: Philip Onyancha
description:
Philip Onyancha confessed to killing 17 people, mostly women, in various parts of Kenya. He claimed to have drunk the blood of his victims. He was arrested in 2010 in Nairobi and faced multiple murder charges.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philip_Onyancha)

title: Pablo Escobar
description:
Pablo Escobar was the leader of the Medellin Cartel and one of the wealthiest criminals in history. He was responsible for thousands of murders, bombings, and kidnappings in Colombia. Killed by Colombian police in Medellin in 1993.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pablo_Escobar)

title: Luis Alfredo Garavito - La Bestia
description:
Luis Alfredo Garavito confessed to the torture, rape, and murder of 147 children across Colombia, making him one of the world's most prolific serial killers. He was sentenced to 1,853 years in prison, reduced to 22 years.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luis_Garavito)

title: Palace of Justice Siege
description:
M-19 guerrillas seized the Palace of Justice in Bogota, taking over 300 hostages including Supreme Court justices. The military retook the building in a controversial assault. Over 100 people died, including 11 justices.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palace_of_Justice_siege)

title: Abimael Guzman Capture
description:
Abimael Guzman was the founder and leader of the Shining Path terrorist organization responsible for an estimated 70,000 deaths in Peru. He was captured in Lima in 1992 and sentenced to life imprisonment. Died in prison in 2021.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abimael_Guzm%C3%A1n)

title: Tarata Street Bombing
description:
The Shining Path detonated a car bomb on Tarata Street in the Miraflores district of Lima, killing 25 people and injuring over 200. It was one of the worst terrorist attacks in Lima's history during the internal conflict.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarata_bombing)

title: Japanese Embassy Hostage Crisis
description:
Members of the MRTA took hundreds of hostages at the Japanese embassy in Lima during a reception. After a 126-day standoff, Peruvian commandos stormed the building, freeing all but one hostage and killing all 14 rebels.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japanese_embassy_hostage_crisis)

title: Murder of Daniel Zamudio
description:
Daniel Zamudio, a 24-year-old gay man, was brutally attacked in a Santiago park by four men. He died from his injuries 25 days later. The case led to Chile passing an anti-discrimination law. The attackers received sentences up to life.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Daniel_Zamudio)

title: Caso Degollados (Caso de los Degollados)
description:
Three Communist Party members - Jose Manuel Parada, Santiago Nattino, and Manuel Guerrero - were kidnapped and had their throats slashed by Carabineros during the Pinochet dictatorship. The case became a symbol of state terror.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caso_Degollados)

title: Rolando Jimenez Case
description:
The case involved a series of femicides in Alto Hospicio near Iquique, Chile. Multiple teenage girls were murdered by Alto Hospicio serial killer later identified as convicted sex offender. The case exposed police negligence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alto_Hospicio_serial_killings)

title: Caracazo Massacre
description:
Mass protests against austerity measures in Caracas were violently suppressed by the Venezuelan government. Hundreds to thousands of civilians were killed by security forces. The exact death toll remains disputed and accountability was never fully established.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracazo)

title: Kidnapping of the Faddoul Brothers
description:
Three brothers from the Faddoul family were kidnapped in Caracas for ransom. Despite the family paying the ransom, the brothers were found dead. The case highlighted Venezuela's epidemic of kidnapping for ransom.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Venezuela)

title: Murder of Monica Spear
description:
Venezuelan actress and beauty queen Monica Spear and her ex-husband were shot and killed during a roadside robbery on a highway in Carabobo state. Their five-year-old daughter survived. Seven suspects were convicted in 2016.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M%C3%B3nica_Spear)

title: Si Ouey Sae Urng
description:
Si Ouey was a Chinese-Thai man convicted of murdering and cannibalizing children in Thailand in the 1950s. He was executed in 1959. His mummified body was displayed at a forensic museum in Bangkok until 2020 when it was cremated.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Si_Ouey)

title: Murder of Vicha Ratanapakdee
description:
The Erawan Shrine bombing in Bangkok on August 17, 2015 killed 20 people and injured over 120. A bomb was detonated at the Hindu shrine at the Ratchaprasong intersection. Two Uyghur men were arrested and convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015_Bangkok_bombing)

title: Nong Chok Mass Shooting
description:
Sergeant Major Jakrapanth Thomma carried out a mass shooting in Nakhon Ratchasima, killing 29 people and injuring 58 at a shopping mall. He was killed by security forces after an 18-hour standoff.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2020_Nakhon_Ratchasima_shootings)
